speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Old John of Gaunt, time-honored Lancaster,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Hast thou, according to thy oath and band,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Brought hither Henry Hereford, thy bold son,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Here to make good the boist’rous late appeal,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Which then our leisure would not let us hear,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Against the Duke of Norfolk, Thomas Mowbray?

speaker: GAUNT
text: I have, my liege.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Tell me, moreover, hast thou sounded him

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: If he appeal the Duke on ancient malice

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Or worthily, as a good subject should,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: On some known ground of treachery in him?

speaker: GAUNT
text: As near as I could sift him on that argument,

speaker: GAUNT
text: On some apparent danger seen in him

speaker: GAUNT
text: Aimed at your Highness, no inveterate malice.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Then call them to our presence.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Face to face

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And frowning brow to brow, ourselves will hear

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The accuser and the accusèd freely speak.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: High stomached are they both and full of ire,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: In rage deaf as the sea, hasty as fire.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Many years of happy days befall

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My gracious sovereign, my most loving liege.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Each day still better other’s happiness

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Until the heavens, envying earth’s good hap,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Add an immortal title to your crown.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: We thank you both. Yet one but flatters us,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: As well appeareth by the cause you come:

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Namely, to appeal each other of high treason.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Cousin of Hereford, what dost thou object

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Against the Duke of Norfolk, Thomas Mowbray?

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: First—heaven be the record to my speech!—

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: In the devotion of a subject’s love,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Tend’ring the precious safety of my prince

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And free from other misbegotten hate,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Come I appellant to this princely presence.—

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Now, Thomas Mowbray, do I turn to thee;

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And mark my greeting well, for what I speak

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My body shall make good upon this earth

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Or my divine soul answer it in heaven.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Thou art a traitor and a miscreant,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Too good to be so and too bad to live,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Since the more fair and crystal is the sky,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The uglier seem the clouds that in it fly.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Once more, the more to aggravate the note,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: With a foul traitor’s name stuff I thy throat,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And wish, so please my sovereign, ere I move,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: What my tongue speaks my right-drawn sword may prove.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Let not my cold words here accuse my zeal.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: ’Tis not the trial of a woman’s war,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: The bitter clamor of two eager tongues,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Can arbitrate this cause betwixt us twain.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: The blood is hot that must be cooled for this.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Yet can I not of such tame patience boast

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: As to be hushed and naught at all to say.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: First, the fair reverence of your Highness curbs me

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: From giving reins and spurs to my free speech,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Which else would post until it had returned

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: These terms of treason doubled down his throat.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Setting aside his high blood’s royalty,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And let him be no kinsman to my liege,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: I do defy him, and I spit at him,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Call him a slanderous coward and a villain,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Which to maintain I would allow him odds

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And meet him, were I tied to run afoot

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Even to the frozen ridges of the Alps

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Or any other ground inhabitable

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Wherever Englishman durst set his foot.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Meantime let this defend my loyalty:

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: By all my hopes, most falsely doth he lie.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Pale trembling coward, there I throw my gage,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Disclaiming here the kindred of the King,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And lay aside my high blood’s royalty,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Which fear, not reverence, makes thee to except.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: If guilty dread have left thee so much strength

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: As to take up mine honor’s pawn, then stoop.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: By that and all the rites of knighthood else

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Will I make good against thee, arm to arm,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: What I have spoke or thou canst worse devise.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: I take it up, and by that sword I swear

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Which gently laid my knighthood on my shoulder,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: I’ll answer thee in any fair degree

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Or chivalrous design of knightly trial;

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And when I mount, alive may I not light

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: If I be traitor or unjustly fight.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: What doth our cousin lay to Mowbray’s charge?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: It must be great that can inherit us

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: So much as of a thought of ill in him.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Look what I speak, my life shall prove it true:

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: That Mowbray hath received eight thousand nobles

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: In name of lendings for your Highness’ soldiers,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The which he hath detained for lewd employments,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Like a false traitor and injurious villain.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Besides I say, and will in battle prove,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Or here or elsewhere to the furthest verge

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: That ever was surveyed by English eye,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: That all the treasons for these eighteen years

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Complotted and contrivèd in this land

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Fetch from false Mowbray their first head and spring.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Further I say, and further will maintain

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Upon his bad life to make all this good,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: That he did plot the Duke of Gloucester’s death,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Suggest his soon-believing adversaries,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And consequently, like a traitor coward,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Sluiced out his innocent soul through streams of blood,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Which blood, like sacrificing Abel’s, cries

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Even from the tongueless caverns of the earth

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To me for justice and rough chastisement.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And, by the glorious worth of my descent,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: This arm shall do it, or this life be spent.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: How high a pitch his resolution soars!—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Thomas of Norfolk, what sayst thou to this?

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: O, let my sovereign turn away his face

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And bid his ears a little while be deaf,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Till I have told this slander of his blood

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: How God and good men hate so foul a liar.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Mowbray, impartial are our eyes and ears.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Were he my brother, nay, my kingdom’s heir,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: As he is but my father’s brother’s son,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Now by my scepter’s awe I make a vow:

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Such neighbor nearness to our sacred blood

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Should nothing privilege him nor partialize

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The unstooping firmness of my upright soul.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: He is our subject, Mowbray; so art thou.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Free speech and fearless I to thee allow.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Then, Bolingbroke, as low as to thy heart,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Through the false passage of thy throat, thou liest.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Three parts of that receipt I had for Calais

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Disbursed I duly to his Highness’ soldiers;

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: The other part reserved I by consent,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: For that my sovereign liege was in my debt

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Upon remainder of a dear account

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Since last I went to France to fetch his queen.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Now swallow down that lie. For Gloucester’s death,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: I slew him not, but to my own disgrace

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Neglected my sworn duty in that case.—

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: For you, my noble Lord of Lancaster,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: The honorable father to my foe,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Once did I lay an ambush for your life,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: A trespass that doth vex my grievèd soul.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: But ere I last received the sacrament,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: I did confess it and exactly begged

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Your Grace’s pardon, and I hope I had it.—

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: This is my fault. As for the rest appealed,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: It issues from the rancor of a villain,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: A recreant and most degenerate traitor,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Which in myself I boldly will defend,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And interchangeably hurl down my gage

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Upon this overweening traitor’s foot,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: To prove myself a loyal gentleman,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Even in the best blood chambered in his bosom;

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: In haste whereof most heartily I pray

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Your Highness to assign our trial day.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Wrath-kindled gentlemen, be ruled by me.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Let’s purge this choler without letting blood.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: This we prescribe, though no physician.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Deep malice makes too deep incision.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Forget, forgive; conclude and be agreed.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Our doctors say this is no month to bleed.—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Good uncle, let this end where it begun;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: We’ll calm the Duke of Norfolk, you your son.

speaker: GAUNT
text: To be a make-peace shall become my age.—

speaker: GAUNT
text: Throw down, my son, the Duke of Norfolk’s gage.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And, Norfolk, throw down his.

speaker: GAUNT
text: When, Harry, when?

speaker: GAUNT
text: Obedience bids I should not bid again.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Norfolk, throw down, we bid; there is no boot.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Myself I throw, dread sovereign, at thy foot.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: My life thou shalt command, but not my shame.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: The one my duty owes, but my fair name,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Despite of death that lives upon my grave,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: To dark dishonor’s use thou shalt not have.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: I am disgraced, impeached, and baffled here,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Pierced to the soul with slander’s venomed spear,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: The which no balm can cure but his heart-blood

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Which breathed this poison.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Rage must be withstood.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Give me his gage. Lions make leopards tame.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Yea, but not change his spots. Take but my shame

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And I resign my gage. My dear dear lord,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: The purest treasure mortal times afford

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Is spotless reputation; that away,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Men are but gilded loam or painted clay.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: A jewel in a ten-times-barred-up chest

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Is a bold spirit in a loyal breast.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Mine honor is my life; both grow in one.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Take honor from me and my life is done.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Then, dear my liege, mine honor let me try.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: In that I live, and for that will I die.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Cousin, throw up your gage. Do you begin.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: O, God defend my soul from such deep sin!

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Shall I seem crestfallen in my father’s sight?

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Or with pale beggar-fear impeach my height

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Before this out-dared dastard? Ere my tongue

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Shall wound my honor with such feeble wrong

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Or sound so base a parle, my teeth shall tear

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The slavish motive of recanting fear

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And spit it bleeding in his high disgrace,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Where shame doth harbor, even in Mowbray’s face.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: We were not born to sue, but to command,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Which, since we cannot do, to make you friends,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Be ready, as your lives shall answer it,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: At Coventry upon Saint Lambert’s day.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: There shall your swords and lances arbitrate

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The swelling difference of your settled hate.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Since we cannot atone you, we shall see

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Justice design the victor’s chivalry.—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Lord Marshal, command our officers-at-arms

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Be ready to direct these home alarms.
